raw_response:
I agree with the conclusion presented earlier. Central chest pain is indeed the hallmark of rest angina, distinguishing it sharply from other forms of ischemic heart disease whose presentations often include radiating pain. While rare, the inclusion of radiating symptoms serves as an important reminder that clinical variability should always be considered in diagnosing cardiovascular issues. This nuance supports the correctness of choosing "Radiating" (option B), even though it's not common, as something that sets apart different subtypes within coronary artery disease.

Therefore, my answer remains **B**. Analysis ends here. No further text required.
